name: telegram
description: Send and manage Telegram bot messages through the Telegram Bot API

envVars.name: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN
envVars.description: Telegram bot token issued by @BotFather, in the form 123456789:ABC-DEF...

tools.name: Send Message
tools.description: Send a text message to a chat, group, or channel the bot has access to

chat_id.description: Unique identifier of the target chat (numeric id from getUpdates) or channel username in the form @channelusername

text.description: Text of the message to send (1-4096 characters after entities parsing)

parse_mode.description: Formatting mode for the text: MarkdownV2, HTML, or Markdown (legacy). Omit for plain text

tools.name: Get Updates
tools.description: Receive incoming updates (messages sent to the bot) via long polling; use it to discover chat ids

offset.description: Identifier of the first update to return; pass the highest received update_id + 1 to confirm earlier updates

limit.description: Maximum number of updates to retrieve (1-100)

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for long polling; 0 returns immediately

tools.name: Get Me
tools.description: Return basic information about the bot itself; useful for verifying the token works

tools.name: Send Photo
tools.description: Send a photo to a chat by passing a publicly reachable HTTP URL or an existing Telegram file_id

chat_id.description: Unique identifier of the target chat or channel username in the form @channelusername

photo.description: HTTP URL of the photo for Telegram to fetch (max 10 MB), or a file_id of a photo already on Telegram servers

caption.description: Photo caption (0-1024 characters after entities parsing)

tools.name: Edit Message Text
tools.description: Edit the text of a message previously sent by the bot

chat_id.description: Unique identifier of the chat containing the message, or channel username in the form @channelusername

message_id.description: Identifier of the message to edit (as returned when the message was sent)

text.description: New text of the message (1-4096 characters after entities parsing)

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Telegram setup

steps.title: Create a bot with @BotFather
steps.description: In any Telegram client (a regular free Telegram account works), open a chat with @BotFather, send /newbot, and follow the prompts to pick a display name and a unique username ending in 'bot'. BotFather replies with the bot token.

steps.title: Store the token
steps.description: Set the token from BotFather as TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN. You can regenerate it at any time with /revoke in @BotFather, which invalidates the old token.

steps.title: Open a chat with the bot
steps.description: Bots cannot message users first. Start a direct chat with your bot (or add it to a group) and send it a message; the chat then appears in getUpdates, where you can read the numeric chat_id used to address messages.

notes: The Telegram Bot API authenticates by embedding the bot token in the request URL path (https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/<method>); it does not accept the token in an HTTP header.
Keep the token secret: anyone who has it fully controls the bot.
